notes: Discriminating eval: eval-1 (mini-repo, zero deps). Baseline treated the absence of dependencies as gap-worthy problems; the skill's 'zero edges is a recorded fact, not an error' framing prevented exactly that. This is where the skill earns its keep.

Non-discriminating eval: eval-2 (workspace:*/file: protocols, dev-only edge, missing manifest) — both configs scored 6/6. The model handles protocol mechanics unaided; the skill's value is behavioral (gap discipline, zero-deps framing), not mechanical.

Shared failure (both configs, eval-0): gaps[] scope is undefined in the skill, so runs invent extra-mandate gaps — convention notes (workspace:* vs '*' — that's B3 convention-detector territory), source-import verification, toolchain checks. Skill should define what belongs in B2 gaps[] (only unreadable/missing manifests).

Performance: with-skill maxi run was the slowest (80.7s, 11 tool uses) because it crawled source imports to 'verify' manifest edges — beyond the manifest-only mandate. Stating 'manifests are the evidence; do not verify imports in source' would cut time and tokens.
Tokens are a wash (+135); time slightly favors the skill (-5.9s mean) driven by eval-1/eval-2 where the skill's tight procedure avoided exploratory wandering.
